rulings.Case Title: ROSE FELIX VS. 925-945 CORBETT HOMEOWNERS ASSOCIATION ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion For Determination Of Good Faith Settlement
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for August 25, 2021 line 1. DEFENDANT JON K. TAKATA CORP. Notice Of Motion For Determination Of Good Faith Settlement is DENIED. No showing made that the amount of settlement is in the ballpark of this defendant's proportional liability. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303; or Phone Dial in: (415) 762-9988; Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified, and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: SALLY L. LIU VS. BRUCE R. BEASLEY ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for August 25, 2021 line 3. PLAINTIFF SALLY LIU Motion For Imposing Sanctions is DENIED. CRC 3.1312 allows both parties to submit proposed orders in a timely fashion. CRC 3.1312 does not provide for imposition of sanctions on the prevailing party for failure to submit a proposed order. Non-prevailing party is authorized to submit an order when the prevailing party fails to do so. CRC 3.1312(d). =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303; or Phone Dial in: (415) 762-9988; Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified, and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: JAMES REID VS. JOHN H. MARBLE JR. ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for August 25, 2021 line 8. DEFENDANT JOHN MARBLE DEMURRER to COMPLAINT is OVERRULED, 5 days to answer. The Court will consider the first 15 pages of the points and authorities submitted in support of the demurrer. Pages 22-55 if the demurrer filing as stricken. Demurrer is overruled, 5 days to answer. Complaint sufficiently states a cause of action, is not uncertain, alleges the existence of a written contract and Plaintiff's standing to proceed. Factual disputes challenging the veracity of Plaintiff's allegations are not properly addressed on a demurrer. Defendant is warned that any future failures to comply with CCP, CRC and SFLR procedural provisions will result in motions being taken off calendar and briefs stricken in their entirety. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303; or Phone Dial in: (415) 762-9988; Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303). Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests.
